title: GPT-5.6 general availability: Sol, Terra, and Luna
author: OpenAI
why_it_matters: OpenAI shipped GPT-5.6 as a three-tier model family and explicitly tied it to Codex, ChatGPT Work, the API, Programmatic Tool Calling, multi-agent beta, max reasoning, ultra mode, and prompt caching.
actionable_takeaway: Route hard Codex and knowledge-work tasks to Sol/max or ultra, everyday automation to Terra, and high-volume summarization to Luna; record the routing choice in each workflow card.

title: GPT-5.6 in ChatGPT, Work, Codex, and API availability matrix
author: OpenAI Help Center
why_it_matters: The help article separates standard ChatGPT conversations from Work, Codex, and API availability, which prevents users from expecting the same model picker everywhere.
actionable_takeaway: Write model-choice instructions as product-specific steps: ChatGPT chat uses Sol by reasoning level, while Codex and Work can expose Sol/Terra/Luna choices.

title: OpenAI API model guidance for GPT-5.6
author: OpenAI Developers
why_it_matters: The model guidance turns GPT-5.6 into concrete API choices: Programmatic Tool Calling, Multi-agent beta, explicit prompt caching, persisted reasoning, max effort, pro mode, and original image detail.
actionable_takeaway: For every agent workflow, decide which stages need tool orchestration, subagents, cache breakpoints, preserved reasoning, or pro mode before adding code.

title: Programmatic Tool Calling guide
author: OpenAI Developers
why_it_matters: PTC lets the model run bounded JavaScript inside a hosted runtime to coordinate eligible tools and reduce intermediate noise, but it is not a replacement for judgment-heavy steps.
actionable_takeaway: Use PTC only for bounded stages with eligible tools, explicit output schema, retry limit, stop condition, and handoff back to model judgment.

title: Multi-agent beta in the Responses API
author: OpenAI Developers
why_it_matters: OpenAI now documents a first-party beta where one GPT-5.6 request can coordinate subagents in parallel and synthesize their work.
actionable_takeaway: Split only naturally parallel work: source collection, fact checking, codebase exploration, implementation, and final synthesis.

title: Codex joins the ChatGPT desktop app
author: OpenAI Learn
why_it_matters: The Codex changelog says Codex is now part of the ChatGPT desktop app on macOS and Windows, with direct Markdown/code editing, PR review, and cross-repository projects.
actionable_takeaway: Treat ChatGPT desktop as the daily control room: edit content, review PRs, and run Codex projects without switching apps.

title: Codex CLI 0.144 adds write-approval mode and Ultra usage warnings
author: OpenAI Learn
why_it_matters: The CLI changelog adds a writes approval mode, interactive MCP authentication, usage-credit clarity, and warnings when Ultra concurrency could increase usage quickly.
actionable_takeaway: Use read-only-by-default approval modes for research automations, and add an explicit cost/usage check before enabling Ultra on broad tasks.

title: GPT-5.6 System Card
author: OpenAI Deployment Safety
why_it_matters: The system card highlights stronger cyber and bio capabilities, stronger safeguards, and a measured concern that agentic coding tasks can sometimes go beyond user intent.
actionable_takeaway: For Codex tasks, keep hard approval boundaries, scoped goals, and post-run diff review, especially around security-sensitive or write-capable operations.

title: Mastering remote engineering work from your phone
author: Thomas Ricouard / OpenAI Developers
why_it_matters: The post frames the phone as an engineering control plane: choose host, workspace, branch or worktree, queue versus steer, review, approve, and organize work without turning the phone into a tiny terminal.
actionable_takeaway: Use iPhone for context selection, steering, approvals, and queue hygiene; keep deep review, publishing, and irreversible actions on Mac.

title: Making private MCP servers reachable without making them public
author: Denys Kurylenko / OpenAI Developers
why_it_matters: The Secure MCP Tunnel architecture keeps private MCP servers behind customer boundaries by using an outbound customer-run client and explicit destination configuration.
actionable_takeaway: Do not expose private tools through ad-hoc public tunnels; prefer outbound-only, inspectable, narrow, configured paths.

title: GitHub Copilot adds OpenAI GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna
author: GitHub Changelog
why_it_matters: GitHub exposing GPT-5.6 in Copilot means the same model-family routing question will show up outside OpenAI-native products.
actionable_takeaway: Keep model-selection notes portable: task difficulty, cost tolerance, tool access, review requirement, and whether the workflow is inside Codex or another IDE agent.

title: Command-line AI coding agents adoption study at Microsoft
author: Emerson Murphy-Hill, Jenna Butler, Alexandra Savelieva
why_it_matters: The study reports organization-scale evidence that command-line coding agents were adopted through social networks and that adopters merged more pull requests, while warning that merged PRs are only a proxy for value.
actionable_takeaway: When evaluating Codex adoption, track peer-visible usage, retention, merged output, review quality, and cost instead of relying on anecdotes.
